premises-NL: If a student misses the lab exam, the lab score is zero.
If the lab score is zero, the student cannot pass the course.
If the final exam score is at least 8 and the student passes the course, the student may receive a B.
The student missed the lab exam.
questions: Can the student receive a B in this course?

What follows from the lab absence?
A. The student can ignore lab work
B. The lab score is zero
C. The final exam score is invalid
D. The course has no lab
explanation: The student missed the lab exam, so the lab score is zero. A zero lab score means the student cannot pass the course, so the student cannot receive a B.
Premise 1 directly states that missing the lab exam makes the lab score zero.
